title: Google adapter constructs ToolCall ids with random UUIDs every parse
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/providers/google.rs:212, src/pipeline.rs:453, src/streaming.rs:308
## Violation
Three sites generate tool-call ids for Gemini using different schemes — pipeline.rs uses format!("call_{}", Uuid::new_v4()), GoogleAdapter::transform_response uses format!("call_{}", Uuid::new_v4()), and streaming::parse_gemini uses format!("gemini_{name}") with no UUID. The format!("gemini_{name}") form is NOT unique — two consecutive bash function-calls in one response collide on id 'gemini_bash', breaking tool_use_id linkage when relayed to Anthropic via convert_messages_to_anthropic which uses the id to bind tool_result back to tool_use. Inconsistent ID schemes also frustrate log correlation.
## Mandated refactor
Lift a single id_generator: fn gemini_tool_id(name: &str, index: usize) -> String into providers/google.rs. Use it in all three sites. Include a counter to guarantee uniqueness even when the model emits the same function name twice in a turn.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
